description: Site Performance — Non-Dashboard Pages: Eliminate N+1 waterfalls, migrate to React Query, add skeleton loaders, memoize derived state, and add lazy loading across all non-dashboard pages

userStories.title: Enrich friend requests endpoint with user profiles
userStories.description: As a user viewing my Friends page, I want friend requests to include displayName and photoURL so the frontend doesn't need separate API calls per request.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /friends/requests response includes displayName and photoURL for each request sender/receiver
Uses batched Firestore getAll() to fetch user profiles in a single read
Gracefully handles missing user profiles (deleted accounts) by returning a fallback display name
Existing API tests updated; new test covers enriched response shape
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Backend-only change. The friends route handler currently returns raw request objects — enrich them before responding.

userStories.title: Add scoped group workouts endpoint for burn buddies
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a /burn-buddies/:id/group-workouts endpoint that returns only workouts between the two buddies instead of all workouts.
acceptanceCriteria: New GET /burn-buddies/:id/group-workouts endpoint returns only group workouts where both buddy participants are involved
Endpoint is protected by requireAuth middleware
Returns empty array if no workouts found
API test covers the new endpoint with mock data
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Backend-only. Currently the frontend fetches GET /group-workouts and filters client-side — this replaces that pattern.

userStories.title: Add scoped group workouts endpoint for burn squads
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a /burn-squads/:id/group-workouts endpoint that returns only workouts for that squad.
acceptanceCriteria: New GET /burn-squads/:id/group-workouts endpoint returns only group workouts associated with that squad
Endpoint is protected by requireAuth middleware
Returns empty array if no workouts found
API test covers the new endpoint with mock data
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Backend-only. Mirrors US-002 pattern but for squads.

userStories.title: Enrich burn squad response with member profiles
userStories.description: As a user viewing a Burn Squad, I want all member profiles included in the squad response so the frontend doesn't make one API call per member.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /burn-squads/:id response includes enriched members array with displayName and photoURL per member
Uses batched Firestore getAll() to fetch member profiles (chunks of 100 for defensive handling)
Gracefully handles missing user profiles with fallback display name
API test covers enriched members in response
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Backend-only. Currently frontend makes individual GET /users/{uid} per squad member — this N+1 is the worst performance issue.

userStories.title: Add React Query hooks for non-dashboard pages
userStories.description: As a developer, I want React Query hooks defined in lib/queries.ts for all non-dashboard data fetching so pages can be migrated incrementally.
acceptanceCriteria: New hooks in apps/web/src/lib/queries.ts: useFriends(), useBurnBuddy(id), useBurnSquad(id), useAccount()
Existing useProfile(uid) hook is verified to work (already defined but unused)
Query key conventions: ['friends'], ['burn-buddy', id], ['burn-squad', id], ['account'], ['profile', uid]
useFriends() fetches /friends, /friends/requests, /burn-buddies, and /burn-buddies/requests in parallel
useBurnBuddy(id) fetches /burn-buddies/:id, partner profile, streaks, and scoped group workouts
useBurnSquad(id) fetches /burn-squads/:id (with enriched members), streaks, and scoped group workouts
useAccount() fetches /users/me with staleTime of 5 minutes
All other hooks use default staleTime of 30 seconds
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This creates the hooks only — pages are migrated in subsequent stories. Hooks should use the shared QueryClient from query-provider.tsx.

userStories.title: Migrate Friends page to React Query with skeleton loader
userStories.description: As a user on the Friends page, I want fast cached loads and a skeleton UI instead of 'Loading...' text.
acceptanceCriteria: Friends page uses useFriends() hook instead of raw useState/useEffect data fetching
All raw useState for friends/requests/loading removed and replaced with hook return values
Frontend no longer makes individual GET /users/{uid} calls to enrich friend requests (uses enriched endpoint from US-001)
FriendsSkeleton component shows pulse placeholder bars for friend list items
Skeleton uses animate-pulse with bg-gray-200 rounded pattern matching DashboardSkeleton
getBurnBuddyStatus() lookups wrapped in useMemo
No 'Loading...' text remains on the page
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on US-001 (enriched friend requests) and US-005 (hooks). Search/action handlers (add friend, accept request) can stay as direct API calls for now.

userStories.title: Migrate Account page to React Query with skeleton loader
userStories.description: As a user on the Account page, I want fast cached loads and a skeleton UI instead of 'Loading...' text.
acceptanceCriteria: Account page uses useAccount() hook instead of raw useState/useEffect for profile loading
Raw useState for profile/dataLoading removed and replaced with hook return values
AccountSkeleton component shows pulse placeholder for profile card
Skeleton uses animate-pulse with bg-gray-200 rounded pattern matching DashboardSkeleton
No 'Loading...' text remains on the page
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on US-005 (hooks). Mutation handlers (save username, upload picture) stay as direct API calls — optimistic updates come in US-012.

userStories.title: Migrate Burn Buddy detail page to React Query with skeleton loader
userStories.description: As a user viewing a Burn Buddy detail page, I want fast cached loads, a skeleton UI, and no over-fetching of workouts.
acceptanceCriteria: Burn Buddy detail page uses useBurnBuddy(id) hook instead of raw useState/useEffect
Uses scoped /burn-buddies/:id/group-workouts endpoint instead of fetching all group workouts
Raw useState for buddy/partner/streaks/workouts/loading removed and replaced with hook return values
BurnBuddySkeleton component shows pulse placeholders for header, stats, and workout list
workoutsThisWeek and workoutsThisMonth calculations wrapped in useMemo
No 'Loading...' text remains on the page
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on US-002 (scoped buddy workouts) and US-005 (hooks).

userStories.title: Migrate Burn Squad detail page to React Query with skeleton loader
userStories.description: As a user viewing a Burn Squad detail page, I want fast cached loads, a skeleton UI, no over-fetching, and no N+1 member lookups.
acceptanceCriteria: Burn Squad detail page uses useBurnSquad(id) hook instead of raw useState/useEffect
Uses scoped /burn-squads/:id/group-workouts endpoint instead of fetching all group workouts
Uses enriched members from /burn-squads/:id response instead of individual /users/{uid} calls
Raw useState for squad/members/streaks/workouts/loading removed and replaced with hook return values
BurnSquadSkeleton component shows pulse placeholders for header, member grid, and workout list
workoutsThisWeek, workoutsThisMonth, and isAdmin calculations wrapped in useMemo
No 'Loading...' text remains on the page
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on US-003 (scoped squad workouts), US-004 (enriched members), and US-005 (hooks). Most complex page migration.

userStories.title: Migrate Profile page to React Query with skeleton loader
userStories.description: As a user viewing another user's profile, I want fast cached loads and a skeleton UI instead of 'Loading...' text.
acceptanceCriteria: Profile page adopts existing useProfile(uid) hook from queries.ts instead of raw useState/useEffect
Raw useState for profile/dataLoading/error removed and replaced with hook return values
ProfileSkeleton component shows pulse placeholder for profile card and stats
No 'Loading...' text remains on the page
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on US-005 (hooks). The useProfile hook already exists but is unused — this story adopts it.

userStories.title: Add optimistic updates for Friends page mutations
userStories.description: As a user on the Friends page, I want the UI to update immediately when I accept or reject a friend request without a full page refetch.
acceptanceCriteria: Accept friend request removes the request from the list immediately using React Query cache mutation (onMutate)
Reject friend request removes the request from the list immediately
On mutation error, cache rolls back to previous state and shows error message
After mutation settles, invalidateQueries(['friends']) triggers background revalidation
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on US-006 (Friends page on React Query). Uses useMutation + onMutate/onError/onSettled pattern.

userStories.title: Add optimistic updates for Account and Profile mutations
userStories.description: As a user, I want my Account and Profile pages to update immediately after saving changes without a full refetch.
acceptanceCriteria: After saving username on Account page, displayed value updates immediately via cache mutation
After uploading profile picture on Account page, avatar updates immediately
After sending a Burn Buddy request from Profile page, button state updates without refetching entire profile
On mutation error, cache rolls back and shows error message
After mutations settle, invalidateQueries triggers background revalidation
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on US-007 (Account on React Query) and US-010 (Profile on React Query).

userStories.title: Add lazy loading to Avatar component
userStories.description: As a user on pages with many avatars (Friends list, Squad members), I want off-screen images to load lazily for faster initial render.
acceptanceCriteria: Avatar component's tag includes loading='lazy' attribute
Avatar component's tag includes decoding='async' for non-blocking decode
No visual change to above-the-fold avatars
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Simple cross-cutting change to apps/web/src/components/Avatar.tsx. No dependencies — can be done anytime.
